downstream_consumers: Any package importing clearScanCache/getScanCacheStats from @coderef/core (will now work where previously broken)

risks.title: Absolute path test fix changes expected behavior
risks.mitigation: Use __dirname-relative path that actually exists in the test fixtures directory

risks.title: Non-existent dir fix might break callers that expect empty array
risks.mitigation: Only fix the test expectation to match current silent behavior — do not change scanner.ts to throw

risks.title: Call-edge fix changes test assertion method
risks.mitigation: Count individual calls in metadata.calls array, which is well-defined in the graph builder

candidates.change: Add clearScanCache and getScanCacheStats to scanner exports on line 156
Fix absolute path (line 189) and non-existent dir expectation (line 246-248)
Fix callEdges.length assertion to count individual calls (line 108)

decision_records.decision: Fix non-existent dir test by updating test expectation to match actual behavior (silent empty array), not by throwing in scanner.ts
decision_records.rationale: Changing scanner.ts to throw would be a behavior change with unknown downstream effects; the test expectation is what's wrong here per the dispatch scope

decision_records.decision: Fix call-edge accuracy test by counting calls within edge metadata, not by changing graph builder aggregation
decision_records.rationale: The graph builder's per-file-pair aggregation is intentional design; the test expectation was written expecting per-call-site edges which is not how the builder works
